name: Ladon Dragon
description: Ladon was a monstrous dragon in Greek mythology, typically depicted as a multi-headed serpent or dragon-like creature. He was tasked with guarding the golden apples in the Garden of the Hesperides, a sacred orchard belonging to Hera, the queen of the gods. Ladon was vigilant and never slept, making him a formidable guardian. The dragon's scales were said to be impenetrable, and its breath could be poisonous. He coiled around the tree bearing the golden apples, ensuring no one could steal them. The eleventh labor of Heracles involved retrieving these golden apples, which ultimately led to Ladon's demise. The myth of Ladon symbolizes the challenges and obstacles that heroes must overcome to achieve their goals, and the importance of vigilance and protection of valuable treasures. While some accounts portray Ladon as a single dragon, others describe him as having hundreds of heads, each speaking in a different voice, making him a truly terrifying guardian. The creature's image is often associated with strength, protection, and the dangers of greed.
subtitle: Guardian of the Golden Apples
appearance: Ladon is typically depicted as a large, serpentine dragon with multiple heads, ranging from one to one hundred, depending on the account. His scales are described as shimmering and impenetrable. Some depictions give him a human-like torso from which the heads emerge. His eyes are often described as glowing with an eerie light, and his breath is said to be toxic and capable of causing instant death. The dragon coils around the tree of the Hesperides, his massive body a formidable obstacle.
habitat: The Garden of the Hesperides, a mythical orchard located far to the west, near the Atlas Mountains.
behavior: Ladon is constantly vigilant and protective of the golden apples. He is fierce and aggressive towards anyone who approaches the garden. Due to his multi-headed nature, he can observe his surroundings from multiple angles simultaneously, making him nearly impossible to sneak past. The dragon is loyal to Hera and dedicated to fulfilling his role as guardian.

keyMyths.title: The Eleventh Labor of Heracles
keyMyths.description: Heracles was tasked with retrieving the golden apples of the Hesperides. He ultimately slew Ladon, either by brute force, poison, or with the help of Atlas, who tricked the dragon.

sources.author: Pseudo-Apollodorus
sources.description: A comprehensive source of Greek myths and legends.
sources.text: A comprehensive source of Greek myths and legends.
